user_request: Draft a suit for damages for defamation against a Defendant who published false and malicious allegations on Facebook damaging the Plaintiff's business reputation. Reproduce the defamatory statements in the body of the plaint. Plead malice, publication to third parties, loss of goodwill, and mental agony. Claim Rs.10,00,000/- as damages. Include prayer for permanent injunction restraining further publication on Facebook or any other social media platform. Draft suitable for filing before the District Court. Plead limitation correctly for defamation suits. Include cause of action paragraph with accrual date, continuing nature of cause of action, valuation, court fee, and proper verification clause.

facts.summary: Plaintiff seeks damages for defamation against a Defendant for publishing false and malicious allegations on Facebook, which damaged the Plaintiff's business reputation. The suit claims Rs. 10,00,000 in damages, mental agony, and loss of goodwill, and seeks a permanent injunction against further publication.

assumptions: Jurisdiction lies with the District Court based on Plaintiff's residence or place of publication
Cause of action is continuing due to online publication

Parties should not plead conclusions of law or of mixed law and fact. It is for the court to declare the law arising

sons are sued for a debt incurred by their deceased father and late manager of the joint family, it is not necessary to

or create the duty. Thus, in a suit for damages for negligence, it is not enough for the plaintiff to state that the 
defendant has been guilty of negligence, without showing in what respect he was negligent and how he became 
bound to use care to prevent injury to others. Negligence means a breach of duty to take due care and caution. The 
plaint therefore, ought to state facts upon which the supposed duty is founded, and the

draft_artifacts.title: PLAINT UNDER THE CIVIL PROCEDURE CODE FOR DAMAGES FOR DEFAMATION AND PERMANENT INJUNCTION
draft_artifacts.text:
IN THE COURT OF THE DISTRICT JUDGE, {{STATE_NAME}}, AT {{CITY}}

CIVIL SUIT NO. ____ OF {{YEAR}}

PLAINTIFF:
{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, aged {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}.

VERSUS

DEFENDANT:
{{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, aged {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}.

PLAINT FOR RECOVERY OF DAMAGES FOR DEFAMATION AND FOR PERMANENT INJUNCTION

1. Parties and background
1.1 The Plaintiff is engaged in business and enjoys goodwill and reputation in the course of such business.
1.2 The Defendant is active on Facebook and has published statements concerning the Plaintiff on Facebook, as pleaded below.

2. Jurisdiction
2.1 This Hon’ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to entertain and try the present suit as the Plaintiff carries on business / has reputation and goodwill within the territorial jurisdiction of this Hon’ble Court at {{CITY}}, and the impugned Facebook publication was accessed/read by third parties within the said territorial jurisdiction, causing harm to the Plaintiff’s reputation and business within this jurisdiction.
2.2 This Hon’ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction as the suit is valued at Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs Only).

3. Defamatory publication on Facebook (verbatim words)
3.1 On or about {{PUBLICATION_DATE}}, the Defendant published on Facebook (on the Defendant’s Facebook profile/page: {{DEFENDANT_FACEBOOK_PROFILE_URL}} / post URL: {{FACEBOOK_POST_URL}}) false and malicious allegations concerning the Plaintiff.
3.2 The Defendant published, inter alia, the following defamatory statements (reproduced verbatim below):

    {{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENTS_VERBATIM}}

(For example, the verbatim block may be presented as:
(i) “…”
(ii) “…”
(iii) “…”)

3.3 The aforesaid statements, in their natural and ordinary meaning and/or by innuendo, are defamatory of the Plaintiff and are calculated to lower the Plaintiff’s reputation in the estimation of right-thinking members of society, including the Plaintiff’s customers, prospective customers, vendors, and business associates.

4. Publication to third parties
4.1 The impugned statements were published on Facebook in a manner visible/accessible to third parties (including the Defendant’s friends/followers and/or the public, as applicable), and were in fact read/accessed by third parties.
4.2 The Plaintiff relies upon screenshots/printouts of the impugned Facebook publication, annexed hereto and marked as Annexure A.
4.3 The Plaintiff relies upon the URL/details/metadata identifying the post/profile, annexed hereto and marked as Annexure B.

5. Falsity and malice
5.1 The impugned statements are false and were made without any lawful justification or reasonable basis.
5.2 The Defendant published the statements with malice and with the intent to injure the Plaintiff’s reputation and business, and/or with reckless disregard for the truth.

6. Harm, loss of goodwill, and mental agony
6.1 By reason of the publication of the impugned defamatory statements, the Plaintiff’s reputation and goodwill have been harmed.
6.2 The Plaintiff has suffered mental agony, humiliation, and distress due to the Defendant’s acts.

7. Damages
7.1 The Plaintiff claims damages of Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs Only) towards loss of reputation, loss of goodwill, impact on business, and mental agony, along with costs of the suit.

8. Permanent injunction
8.1 The Plaintiff apprehends that the Defendant may continue to publish and/or repeat the defamatory allegations on Facebook or other social media platforms.
8.2 The Plaintiff therefore seeks a decree of permanent injunction restraining the Defendant and all persons acting on Defendant’s behalf from publishing, circulating, reposting, or otherwise communicating the impugned defamatory statements (or any substantially similar defamatory content) concerning the Plaintiff on Facebook or any other social media platform or through any other mode of publication.

9. Cause of action
9.1 The cause of action first arose on {{PUBLICATION_DATE}} when the Defendant first published the impugned defamatory statements on Facebook.
9.2 The cause of action continues/recurs so long as the impugned publication remains available on Facebook and/or is shared/reposted and continues to cause harm to the Plaintiff.

10. Limitation
10.1 The present suit is filed within limitation. The suit for damages for defamation has been instituted within one year from the date of the impugned publication, i.e., {{PUBLICATION_DATE}}.
10.2 The suit is being filed on {{FILING_DATE}} and is therefore within one year from {{PUBLICATION_DATE}}. (Limitation Act, 1963, s. 3 applies to bar suits filed beyond limitation.)

11. Valuation and Court Fee
11.1 For the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee, the Plaintiff values the suit at Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs Only), being the amount of damages claimed.
11.2 The requisite court fee as per the applicable court-fee law in force in {{STATE_NAME}} on the above valuation is Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} and shall be affixed/paid at the time of filing.

PRAYER
In view of the facts stated above, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon’ble Court be pleased to:

(a) Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for damages of Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs Only);

(b) Pass a decree of permanent injunction restraining the Defendant and all persons acting on Defendant’s behalf from publishing, circulating, reposting, or otherwise communicating any defamatory material concerning the Plaintiff on Facebook or any other social media platform or through any other mode of publication, including the verbatim statements pleaded in paragraph 3 above and/or any substantially similar defamatory content;

(c) Award costs of the suit to the Plaintiff; and

(d) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon’ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS / ENCLOSURES
Annexure A: Screenshots/printouts of the impugned Facebook post(s) dated {{PUBLICATION_DATE}}.

blocking_issues.issue: Limitation is not pleaded correctly for a civil defamation damages suit; the draft leaves limitation period and accrual date as placeholders and does not plead the required one-year period from the date of publication.
blocking_issues.fix: Replace the limitation paragraph with a clear pleading that the suit is filed within one year from the first publication date ({{PUBLICATION_DATE}}), and state filing date ({{FILING_DATE}}) is within one year; avoid citing an unsupported Limitation Act schedule/article number.

blocking_issues.issue: Jurisdictional pleadings are missing/insufficient for a plaint intended for the District Court (territorial and pecuniary jurisdiction not pleaded).
blocking_issues.fix: Insert a dedicated paragraph pleading territorial jurisdiction (place of plaintiff’s business/reputation impact and where the impugned Facebook post was accessed) and pecuniary jurisdiction based on suit valuation Rs. 10,00,000, with appropriate placeholders.
blocking_issues.location: After party description / before merits paragraphs

blocking_issues.issue: Defamatory words are not reproduced verbatim in the body; the draft uses a single placeholder instead of setting out the exact statements as required for a defamation plaint.
blocking_issues.fix: Insert the defamatory statements verbatim in quoted form and itemised (Statement 1, Statement 2, etc.). Use a placeholder only for the verbatim text block (e.g., {{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENTS_VERBATIM}}) to be replaced before filing.

blocking_issues.issue: Document reference label mismatch: the List of Documents includes Annexure A and Annexure B but the body does not refer to these annexures at all.
blocking_issues.fix: Add explicit references in the body to Annexure A/Annexure B (e.g., screenshots/URL details), and ensure the annexure descriptions match the references.
blocking_issues.location: Paragraphs 1–3 and Paragraph 9 (List of Documents / Enclosures)

non_blocking_issues.issue: Verification appears twice (a verification paragraph in the body and another verification block at the end), creating redundancy and potential inconsistency.
non_blocking_issues.fix: Keep a single verification clause at the end and delete the earlier verification paragraph, or clearly distinguish them (preferred: single end verification).
non_blocking_issues.location: Paragraph 7 and the later “Verification:” block near the end

non_blocking_issues.issue: Prayer clause includes costs twice (in clause (a) and again in clause (d)), which is redundant.
non_blocking_issues.fix: Retain costs in one place (either within clause (a) or as a separate final clause) and delete the duplicate.

non_blocking_issues.issue: Injunction prayer uses mixed singular/plural/neutral pronouns (“Defendant, its agents...”) which may be inconsistent if Defendant is an individual.
non_blocking_issues.fix: Replace with neutral phrasing (“Defendant, his/her/its agents…”, or “Defendant and persons acting on Defendant’s behalf”).

unsupported_statements.statement: “thereby causing normal customers and potential clients to withdraw from dealing with the Plaintiff”
unsupported_statements.reason: No specific facts, instances, dates, customer identities, or transactional details were provided in the input to support this factual assertion (it may be pleaded, but is not supported by the provided context).

unsupported_statements.statement: “The Plaintiff has suffered loss of goodwill and opportunities”
unsupported_statements.reason: No supporting particulars (contracts lost, revenue drop, enquiries cancelled, etc.) were provided in the input.

unsupported_statements.statement: “The statements were published with malice… intended to injure the Plaintiff’s reputation”
unsupported_statements.reason: Malice is pleaded as a conclusion without supporting particulars in the provided facts (e.g., prior disputes, knowledge of falsity, refusal to delete, prior animus).

unsupported_statements.statement: “The cause of action is continuing in nature due to ongoing publication and availability…”
unsupported_statements.reason: The input does not provide facts about continued availability (still accessible as of which date), continued shares/re-publications, or continuing harm particulars; also, the legal characterization may require careful fact-anchoring.

final_artifacts.title: PLAINT UNDER THE CIVIL PROCEDURE CODE FOR DAMAGES FOR DEFAMATION AND PERMANENT INJUNCTION
final_artifacts.text:
IN THE COURT OF THE DISTRICT JUDGE, {{STATE_NAME}}, AT {{CITY}}

CIVIL SUIT NO. ____ OF {{YEAR}}

PLAINTIFF:
{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, aged {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}.

VERSUS

DEFENDANT:
{{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, aged {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}.

PLAINT FOR RECOVERY OF DAMAGES FOR DEFAMATION AND FOR PERMANENT INJUNCTION

1. Parties and background
1.1 The Plaintiff is engaged in business and enjoys goodwill and reputation in the course of such business.
1.2 The Defendant is active on Facebook and has published statements concerning the Plaintiff on Facebook, as pleaded below.

2. Jurisdiction
2.1 This Hon’ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to entertain and try the present suit as the Plaintiff carries on business / has reputation and goodwill within the territorial jurisdiction of this Hon’ble Court at {{CITY}}, and the impugned Facebook publication was accessed/read by third parties within the said territorial jurisdiction, causing harm to the Plaintiff’s reputation and business within this jurisdiction.
2.2 This Hon’ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction as the suit is valued at Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs Only).

3. Defamatory publication on Facebook (verbatim words)
3.1 On or about {{PUBLICATION_DATE}}, the Defendant published on Facebook (on the Defendant’s Facebook profile/page: {{DEFENDANT_FACEBOOK_PROFILE_URL}} / post URL: {{FACEBOOK_POST_URL}}) false and malicious allegations concerning the Plaintiff.
3.2 The Defendant published, inter alia, the following defamatory statements (reproduced verbatim below):

    {{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENTS_VERBATIM}}

(For example, the verbatim block may be presented as:
(i) “…”
(ii) “…”
(iii) “…”)

3.3 The aforesaid statements, in their natural and ordinary meaning and/or by innuendo, are defamatory of the Plaintiff and are calculated to lower the Plaintiff’s reputation in the estimation of right-thinking members of society, including the Plaintiff’s customers, prospective customers, vendors, and business associates.

4. Publication to third parties
4.1 The impugned statements were published on Facebook in a manner visible/accessible to third parties (including the Defendant’s friends/followers and/or the public, as applicable), and were in fact read/accessed by third parties.
4.2 The Plaintiff relies upon screenshots/printouts of the impugned Facebook publication, annexed hereto and marked as Annexure A.
4.3 The Plaintiff relies upon the URL/details/metadata identifying the post/profile, annexed hereto and marked as Annexure B.

5. Falsity and malice
5.1 The impugned statements are false and were made without any lawful justification or reasonable basis.
5.2 The Defendant published the statements with malice and with the intent to injure the Plaintiff’s reputation and business, and/or with reckless disregard for the truth.

6. Harm, loss of goodwill, and mental agony
6.1 By reason of the publication of the impugned defamatory statements, the Plaintiff’s reputation and goodwill have been harmed.
6.2 The Plaintiff has suffered mental agony, humiliation, and distress due to the Defendant’s acts.

7. Damages
7.1 The Plaintiff claims damages of Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs Only) towards loss of reputation, loss of goodwill, impact on business, and mental agony, along with costs of the suit.

8. Permanent injunction
8.1 The Plaintiff apprehends that the Defendant may continue to publish and/or repeat the defamatory allegations on Facebook or other social media platforms.
8.2 The Plaintiff therefore seeks a decree of permanent injunction restraining the Defendant and all persons acting on Defendant’s behalf from publishing, circulating, reposting, or otherwise communicating the impugned defamatory statements (or any substantially similar defamatory content) concerning the Plaintiff on Facebook or any other social media platform or through any other mode of publication.

9. Cause of action
9.1 The cause of action first arose on {{PUBLICATION_DATE}} when the Defendant first published the impugned defamatory statements on Facebook.
9.2 The cause of action continues/recurs so long as the impugned publication remains available on Facebook and/or is shared/reposted and continues to cause harm to the Plaintiff.

10. Limitation
10.1 The present suit is filed within limitation. The suit for damages for defamation has been instituted within one year from the date of the impugned publication, i.e., {{PUBLICATION_DATE}}.
10.2 The suit is being filed on {{FILING_DATE}} and is therefore within one year from {{PUBLICATION_DATE}}. (Limitation Act, 1963, s. 3 applies to bar suits filed beyond limitation.)

11. Valuation and Court Fee
11.1 For the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee, the Plaintiff values the suit at Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs Only), being the amount of damages claimed.
11.2 The requisite court fee as per the applicable court-fee law in force in {{STATE_NAME}} on the above valuation is Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} and shall be affixed/paid at the time of filing.

PRAYER
In view of the facts stated above, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon’ble Court be pleased to:

(a) Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for damages of Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs Only);

(b) Pass a decree of permanent injunction restraining the Defendant and all persons acting on Defendant’s behalf from publishing, circulating, reposting, or otherwise communicating any defamatory material concerning the Plaintiff on Facebook or any other social media platform or through any other mode of publication, including the verbatim statements pleaded in paragraph 3 above and/or any substantially similar defamatory content;

(c) Award costs of the suit to the Plaintiff; and

(d) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon’ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS / ENCLOSURES
Annexure A: Screenshots/printouts of the impugned Facebook post(s) dated {{PUBLICATION_DATE}}.
Annexure B: Details/URL/metadata identifying the Defendant’s Facebook profile/page and the impugned post(s) ({{DEFENDANT_FACEBOOK_PROFILE_URL}}; {{FACEBOOK_POST_URL}}).

VERIFICATION
I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the above-named Plaintiff, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 11 are true and correct to my knowledge and belief, and that nothing material has been concealed therefrom.

Verified at {{PLACE}} on this {{DATE}}.
